name: The Farmers State Bank
description_short: Farmers State Bank of Quinton is a community bank established in 1908, offering personal and business checking accounts, mortgages, business loans, and online banking services with FDIC insurance protection.
description_long: Farmers State Bank of Quinton has been serving its community since 1908, establishing itself as a long-standing financial institution with over a century of banking experience. The bank emphasizes its historical commitment to providing quality banking products and services to help customers meet their financial goals. Operating as a traditional community bank, it maintains multiple physical locations and combines in-person service with modern digital banking capabilities. The institution is FDIC-insured, meaning customer deposits are backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government up to applicable limits. The bank offers a comprehensive suite of financial products including personal checking accounts with multiple options to match different customer needs, online and mobile banking platforms, online bill pay with one-time and recurring payment options, and debit card services with fraud protection through Kasasa Protect. On the lending side, Farmers State Bank provides business loans for inventory, equipment, and agricultural expansion (specifically mentioning cattle herd expansion), business checking accounts designed for enterprises of various sizes, and mortgage loans for home purchase with dedicated lending staff. The bank also offers currency exchange services through their online Change Order form and provides customer support through multiple channels including phone, email contact forms, and physical branch locations. Farmers State Bank distinguishes itself through its rural and agricultural focus, explicitly marketing business loans for farming operations and cattle herds, which suggests deep community roots in agricultural areas. The institution emphasizes accessibility, offering screen-reader compatibility and detailed accessibility guidance for customers with disabilities. The bank provides both traditional branch banking and modern digital conveniences, bridging the gap between personalized service and technological convenience. Their longevity since 1908 implies stability and deep community integration. As a regional community bank, Farmers State Bank may have limited product sophistication compared to larger national institutions, and specific information about interest rates, fees, minimum balances, and loan terms is not available on their homepage. The website does not prominently display APY rates for savings products, competitive loan rates, or detailed fee structures. While they offer standard banking services competently, customers seeking specialized financial products, investment services, or extensive digital innovation may find larger institutions more suitable.
services: Personal checking accounts with multiple account tiers
Online and mobile banking platforms with password reset capability
Online bill pay with recurring and one-time payment options
Business checking accounts for small to large enterprises
Business loans for inventory, equipment, and agricultural expansion
Mortgage loans for home purchase with dedicated lending staff
Debit card services with Kasasa Protect fraud protection
Online currency exchange and change order services
Customer contact and support services through web forms
Lost or stolen debit card reporting and replacement
Multi-location branch network with published hours
pros: FDIC-insured deposits backed by full faith and credit of U.S. Government
Established institution with 115+ years of community banking history since 1908
Kasasa Protect fraud protection included with debit card services
Multiple checking account options designed for different personal banking needs
Business loans specifically tailored for agricultural operations and equipment financing
Online bill pay with both one-time and recurring automatic payment options
Dedicated mortgage lending team to assist with home purchase financing
Website accessibility features including screen-reader mode and detailed accessibility guidance
cons: Limited geographic reach as a regional community bank, not available nationwide
Website homepage does not display specific APY rates, interest rates, or fee schedules
No information provided about minimum account balances, monthly fees, or overdraft policies
Limited product offerings compared to major national banks—no investment services or wealth management mentioned
Minimal details about loan terms, APR ranges, or qualification requirements for mortgages and business loans
best_for: Rural and agricultural business owners seeking loans for farming equipment and livestock operations
Homebuyers in the Quinton area looking for personalized mortgage lending with local expertise
Community members preferring traditional branch banking with established local relationships
Small business owners seeking business checking accounts and working capital loans
diagnosis: Farmers State Bank is best for individuals and agricultural business owners in the Quinton area seeking traditional community banking with FDIC protection and personalized local service. The main caveat is that prospective customers must contact the bank directly for specific rates, fees, and loan terms, as this critical information is not disclosed on the public website, and the bank's regional footprint limits accessibility for those outside their service area.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
